title: Vaccine safety and effectiveness (Described video) - Canada.ca
content:
## Vaccine safety and effectiveness (Described video)
Current position: 00:00:00
Total time: 00:02:49

## Transcript - Vaccine safety and effectiveness
Narration: Vaccine safety and effectiveness.
Text on screen: Vaccine Safety and Effectiveness
[A vaccine vial appears inside a circle.]
Narration: How do vaccines get approved?
Text on screen: How do vaccines get approved?
[A magnifying glass appears over the vaccine vial. A question mark appears.]
Narration: And who monitors them in Canada? Let's take a look.
Text on screen: Who monitors vaccines in Canada?
Narration: Extensive regulations exist to evaluate and monitor the safety, effectiveness and quality of vaccines in Canada.
Text on screen: Safety, effectiveness, and quality of vaccines are closely monitored.
[The vaccine vial moves to the side of the screen and checkmarks appear alongside it.]
Narration: Strict protocols are required at every step of vaccine research and development.
Text on screen: Strict protocols
[A gloved hand holds a vaccine vial over a checklist on a clipboard. A shield with a with a medical cross on it travels around the circle. Checkmarks appear on the list.]
Narration: To be approved by Health Canada, a vaccine must show it is safe, effective and of high quality,
Text on screen: Safe; Effective; High Quality
[A vaccine vial appears in a circle. A red line scans the vial, as checkmarks appear beside the text on screen as they are spoken. A check mark and sparkling stars appear on the vaccine vial.]
Narration: and that its benefits outweigh the risks, both when it is used on its own and when given with other vaccines.
Text on screen: On its own and with other vaccines.
[A second vaccine vial pops up in the circle. A line orbits the vials.]
Narration: After approval, Canada has a well-established vaccine safety surveillance system
Text on screen: A Canada-wide safety surveillance system.
[We zoom out and many rows of vaccine vials appear in the circle. As they move across, a circle with a checkmark appears inside the larger circle.]
Narration: where Health Canada works with the Public Health Agency of Canada and the vaccine manufacturers, in cooperation with partner agencies in the provinces, territories, and other countries, to monitor the safety and effectiveness of all approved vaccines.
[Buildings with Canadian flags pop up and a series of dots and red lines connect them together while the sun rises in the background. A vaccine vial and a shield with a check mark appear on the circle's edge. A map of Canada appears in a circle. It zooms out to become a slowly spinning globe.]
Narration: The safety of vaccines is carefully monitored, starting early in product development, and continuing for as long as a vaccine is used.
Text on screen: Monitoring takes place during development and continues as long as the vaccine is used.
[A vaccine vial appears in the circle. A green circle with a with a check mark appears on the vaccine vial.]
Narration: If you have any questions about your vaccines, you can talk to your health care provider or your local public health department.
Text on screen: Questions?
[A cell phone appears with a medical cross shown on it. The phone vibrates.]
Narration: To find out more, visit Canada.ca/childhood-vaccines.
Text on screen: Find out more: Canada.ca/childhood-vaccines
[Canada wordmark appears and fades to black.]
